rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MALEE TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues (last updated and e-mailed to counsel on 04/25/2025) remain unaddressed: 1) Notice must be given to the conservatee. Since this matter was initially set for hearing in August 2024, the conservatee has not received notice of any kind, including notice of each superseded petition. 2) Notice was required to be given after (not before) the second amended petition was filed. See LRSF 14.14(A). New notice of the continued hearing must be given. 3) Petitioner's verification must refer to the Second (instead of First) Amended First Account. 4) The probate referee must sign off on the corrected final inventory and appraisal filed on 02/03/2025. The corrected partial no. 1 inventory and appraisal filed on 06/05/2024 should also be further corrected to state the same opening cash balance. 5) UPS and OMC shares listed on the supplemental inventory and appraisal must be appraised by the probate referee. See Probate Code section 2613 re the valuation date. 6) Notice of the filing of the supplemental inventory and appraisal must be given on the mandatory Judicial Council form GC-042. See sections 2613 & 2610(a). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

The surviving settlor recorded two instruments confirming that title to the subject real property should be in his individual name but still choosing to not record any instruments transferring the subject real property back into the trust. b. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERT D. DOCKENDORFF 2005 REVOCABLE TRUST, AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Invalidating Trust Restatement, Removal And Replacement Of Trustee, Financial Elder Abuse, Return Of Trust Assetts, Imposition Of Constructive Trust, Prohibiting Trustee From Using Trust Funds, Damags, Surcharge, And Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/15/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 5/6/25 cannot be true. The hearing was not continued to 6/10/25 until 4/10/25, so notice of the 6/10/25 hearing could not have been mailed on 4/6/25. (2) A courtesy copy of the petition still has not been provided to the examiner, so it has only been cursorily reviewed. It appears that some of the pages of the 2024 amended and restated trust were cut off...? (3) Paul Melbostad was named as the alternate successor trustee after either Byron Norris or Joey Jones. Did he decline to serve? Please provide courtesy copies of the PETITION and the OBJECTIONS and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/1/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IMOGENE B. GIELING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/8/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "original" will filed on 4/29/25 is a photocopy, not the original. [LRSF 14.20.] (2) If the petitioner is the trustee of the devisee trust, item 8 of the petition must clearly identify all of the beneficiaries of the trust. [LRSF 14.18 C.2.] (3) There is no such pleading called "amendment/supplement." [CRC 7.3.] If any portion of a pleading amends the underlying petition, it is an amendment. An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] Please provide a revised proposed order (re a LOST will unless the original will is found and filed) and courtesy copies of the any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/24/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A supplement to the petition should have been filed to include Clifford Hansen in item 8 of the petition with his date of death. A copy of his death certificate was not required to be filed if the petitioner simply verified his supplement with Mr. Hansen's date of death. Notice of an amendment to a petition or a fully amended petition is not an "amended notice" of the original petition.)

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/05/2025 to be considered with the related petition. The following issues must be addressed prior to the continued hearing 1) Any party lodging a proposed order in a contested matter must file proof of service of the proposed order on all parties entitled to notice of the petition (LRSF 14.7.A.4). 2) The names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent trust beneficiaries must be listed in the petition (CRC 7.902).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUSAN SO-PIK LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/9/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 2b of the petition is blank. (2) Please file a copy of Letters certified within 60 days evidencing the fiduciary capacity of Benson Lee and Judy Leong to act on Betty Lee's behalf. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/25/25. (A supplement, signed and verified by the petitioner, providing the missing information and supporting Letters should suffice.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The Declaration of Real Property has not been required for many years.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HERBERT VILLAFLORES ARCEO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 6/16/26. No appearance will be necessary on that date if either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200 et seq is filed at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER ALLEN BROSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 07/07/2026.
